scope_note: QA Inert/Split/Ramified Prime Classification cert [386]. Primary sources: Neukirch (1999) Algebraic Number Theory ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8 §I.8 (splitting of primes in quadratic fields, Legendre symbol criterion for Q(sqrt(d))); Wall (1960) Fibonacci primitive roots doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano periods, GF(9) generator); Ireland & Rosen (1990) A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.5 (Legendre symbol, quadratic reciprocity). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for Z[phi]=O_{Q(sqrt(5))} where phi^2=phi+1, every prime p classifies as inert/split/ramified exactly as determined by the root count of x^2-x-1 mod p; (C1) exhaustive classification of primes <=50; (C2) inert <-> p mod 5 in {2,3}, split <-> p mod 5 in {1,4}, for all primes <=200; (C3) phi is a primitive element of GF(4) and GF(9) (inert p=2,3); (C4) CRT idempotents for p=11 (split): Z[phi]/(11)=F_11 x F_11; (C5) nilpotent element for p=5 (ramified): (2,1)^2=(0,0) mod 5. Theorem NT: all arithmetic on exact integers; no float state. orbit_family on (b, e, m) is not called directly; orbit geometry is derived from ring-theoretic structure. Extends cert [385] (orbit = prime ideal filtration) to the full inert/split/ramified trichotomy. Derived 2026-06-11.
